title: underground earthy mexican poblano sopes
description: a hearty earthy mexican dish featuring clear honey and poblano peppers, served in hot-pot vessels. perfect for a mid-day meal or a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: ancho chili powder
ingredients.notes: use a mild or medium hot chili powder depending on your preference.

ingredients.name: poblano peppers
ingredients.notes: roast the peppers in the oven at 400°f (200°c) for 10-15 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.

ingredients.name: ground cumin
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality cumin for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: clear honey
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality local honey for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: mince the garlic for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: dice the onion for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality coconut oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality black pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: masa harina
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality masa harina for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: warm water
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality filtered water for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: grilled chicken
ingredients.notes: grill the chicken in a hot skillet with a little bit of coconut oil until cooked through.

instructions: soak the ancho chili powder in 1/2 cup of hot water for 10-15 minutes, or until it is soft and pliable.
heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, and cook the diced onion and minced garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the roasted poblano peppers to the skillet, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
add the cumin, honey, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
in a large bowl, mix together the masa harina and warm water until a dough forms.
knead the dough for 5-10 minutes, until it is smooth and pliable.
divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces, and shape each piece into a ball.
roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
heat a large skillet or hot pot vessel over medium-high heat, and cook the dough circles for 1-2 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
top each sopes with the grilled chicken, a spoonful of the ancho chili pepper mixture, and a sprinkle of cilantro, if desired.
